macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $70K Amid Weakening Momentum and ETF Outflows
items.summary: Bitcoin has dropped below $70,000, entering a phase of cautious reassessment with spot Bitcoin ETFs recording $171 million in net outflows yesterday, including $41.9 million from BlackRock’s IBIT. The market shows range-bound trading influenced by geopolitical risks and macroeconomic pressures.[1][3]

items.title: US Regulators Issue New Crypto Asset Classification Framework
items.summary: American regulators have clarified the status of crypto assets, distinguishing between digital goods, stablecoins, investment contracts, and other tokens, reducing uncertainty and aiding institutional strategies. This shift supports tokenization of real assets and lowers the discount for major tokens like Bitcoin.[1]

items.title: Ethereum Corrects 6% to Retest $2,050 Support
items.summary: Ethereum has declined around 6% in the past 24 hours, retesting $2,050 amid reduced decentralized exchange activity and lagging broader market performance. It struggles to hold above $2,400 due to contracting trading volumes.[3]

items.title: Strategy's Stretch Product Attracts 80% Retail Investors Seeking Low-Volatility Yields
items.summary: Strategy revealed that 80% of holders of its low-volatility digital credit product Stretch (STRC) are retail investors, drawn to its 11.5% annualized dividend; the firm raised $1.2 billion in March to buy Bitcoin and plans $21 billion more. This indicates strong retail demand for stable crypto exposure despite Bitcoin's distance from its all-time high.[3]

items.title: Somnia Blockchain Appoints New CEO, CMO, and COO
items.summary: Somnia has appointed a new CEO, CMO, and COO, marking key leadership changes amid ongoing developments in the blockchain space. Bitcoin holds around $70K with range-bound trading dominated by geopolitical risks.[2]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Washington Governor Signs Landmark AI Transparency and Safety Bills
items.summary: Gov. Bob Ferguson signed two AI-related bills into law, including SB 1786 requiring provenance data in AI-generated media and HB 2592 mandating state agencies identify AI implementation opportunities.

items.title: Meta Introduces TRIBE v2 Foundation Model for Brain Activity Prediction
items.summary: Meta released TRIBE v2, a foundation model trained on 500+ hours of fMRI recordings from 700+ people to predict human brain responses to visual and audio stimuli with zero-shot capabilities.

items.title: Google Introduces Bayesian Teaching Method for LLM Probability Updates
items.summary: Google researchers developed a training method that teaches large language models to update beliefs and probabilities when new evidence appears, addressing a key limitation in LLM agents.

items.title: AI2 Releases Olmo Hybrid with 2× Data Efficiency
items.summary: AI2 released Olmo Hybrid, a 7B-parameter open model combining transformer attention with linear recurrent layers, achieving the same accuracy as Olmo 3 using 49% fewer tokens.

items.title: Google Expands Gemini 3.1 Flash Live and Search Live Globally
items.summary: Google announced global expansion of Search Live and released Gemini 3.1 Flash Live with improved audio AI capabilities for more natural and reliable interactions.

items.title: futurepresent VC Launches $300M AI Startup Fund
items.summary: German-US venture capital firm futurepresent debuted with a $300M fund already deployed across 14 AI startups focusing on infrastructure, robotics, and complex industries.

items.title: Colleges Adopting AI Faster Than Governance Structures Can Keep Pace
items.summary: A new report reveals 74% of faculty report students using AI for essays, but most institutions lack formal policies as universities rapidly integrate AI into admissions, curriculum, and student support.

items.title: AI Drug Discovery Demands New Regulatory Framework
items.summary: Industry experts call for regulatory adaptation as artificial intelligence accelerates drug discovery, requiring updated frameworks to keep pace with technological advancement.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Partner Preview March 2026 Showcases 19 Games with 7 World Premieres
items.summary: Microsoft's Xbox Partner Preview featured 19 third-party games, including 7 world premieres like Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish (Summer 2027), Wuthering Waves (July 2026), and Super Meat Boy 3D (March 31, 2026). 14 titles will launch day-one on Xbox Game Pass, with demos and betas announced.

items.title: Screen Australia Invests $1.4M in 26 Local Game Projects
items.summary: Screen Australia announced $1.4 million funding for 26 Australian game projects focused on local stories and creators, boosting smaller teams and industry growth.

items.title: Sony Closes Dark Outlaw Games
items.summary: Sony has shut down Dark Outlaw Games, as discussed in recent gaming headlines alongside Marathon's PC popularity and the Xbox Partner Preview.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Uber Invests Up to $1.25 Billion in Rivian for EV Fleet Expansion
items.summary: Uber is backing U.S. electric vehicle maker Rivian with up to $1.25 billion to launch around 50,000 vehicles, marking a major partnership in the EV sector.

items.title: Bristol-Led Tech Network Launches UK-China Trade Programme
items.summary: Tech West England is spearheading a new initiative to connect South West UK tech firms with global opportunities in China.

items.title: RoboSense Posts First Quarterly Profit on LiDAR Tech Strength
items.summary: LiDAR technology firm RoboSense reported its first quarterly profit driven by new digital LiDAR innovations and expansion into robotics.

items.title: Jury Orders Meta to Pay $375 Million for Failing to Protect Children
items.summary: A New Mexico jury ruled Meta liable for not warning users of risks and inadequate protection against sexual predators targeting children on its platforms.

items.title: Meta and Google Found Liable in California Social Media Addiction Case
items.summary: A California jury held Meta and YouTube (Google) responsible for addictive platforms contributing to mental health harms in young users.

items.title: Apple Announces WWDC for June 8
items.summary: Apple has scheduled its annual Worldwide Developers Conference to begin on June 8.

items.title: Roseman University Launches 120,000 sq ft Life Science Incubator
items.summary: Roseman Bioventures, a new 120,000-square-foot incubator for life sciences, has been launched by Roseman University.
